forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: de80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Spatial nav: beam test starves cross-region moves — score all directional candidates, not just in-beam'
+---
+## What
+
+From the LeftNav (narrow left strip, near the top of the window), pressing Right doesn't reach the first visible grid row selector or board column. Same failure mode in other "cross-region" moves where the source rect doesn't vertically overlap any candidate-to-the-right that's "in the beam."
+
+### Root cause
+
+`swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs:118-128` — `is_in_beam`:
+
+```rust
+fn is_in_beam(source: &Rect, candidate: &Rect, direction: Direction) -> bool {
+    match direction {
+        Direction::Right | Direction::Left => {
+            candidate.y < source.bottom() && candidate.bottom() > source.y
+        }
+        // ...
+    }
+}
+```
+
+For Left/Right, this requires **any** vertical overlap (≥1px) between source and candidate. If the LeftNav button is at `y:100-140` and the first grid row selector is at `y:200-236`, there's zero overlap — the row selector is **not** "in-beam."
+
+`find_cardinal` (`spatial_nav.rs:194-224`) then pools candidates:
+
+```rust
+// simplified
+let pool = if in_beam.is_empty() { &out_beam } else { &in_beam };
+pool.iter().min_by_key(score)
+```
+
+The out-of-beam fallback **only fires when `in_beam.is_empty()`**. In practice, there's almost always at least one in-beam candidate to the right of the LeftNav (e.g. any toolbar button, header cell, or scope that happens to vertically overlap), so `in_beam` is non-empty → `out_beam` is silently discarded → the row selector (in `out_beam`) is never scored → Right from LeftNav lands on a random small target instead of the main content.
+
+Same culling exists in the JS shim at `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts:136-148`, so the parity fence holds — both implementations are wrong together.
+
+### Fix direction — score everything, weight in-beam
+
+The pool-split is a hard cutoff that loses information. The scoring formula (`13 * major² + minor²` at `spatial_nav.rs:136-140`) already penalizes large minor-axis offsets heavily enough that an in-beam candidate with a small major distance will beat a vertically-distant out-of-beam candidate in most cases. The "in-beam wins even if absurdly far" behavior is the bug.
+
+Replace the pool-split with a unified ranking:
+
+1. All candidates that pass `is_in_direction` (strict: candidate's near edge >= source's far edge in the travel direction) are scored.
+2. Scoring weights the minor axis: in-beam candidates score their beam-relative minor (center distance inside the overlap), out-of-beam candidates score the full center distance (unchanged — already the case at `spatial_nav.rs:140`).
+3. Optionally, multiply out-of-beam scores by a penalty constant (e.g. 1.5×) to mildly prefer in-beam; but the minor-axis penalty usually dominates anyway.
+4. Pick `min_by_key(score)` across the unified pool.
+
+This preserves the preference for beam-aligned candidates (they score lower because minor is smaller) without starving cross-region moves when no in-beam candidate is close.
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` — rewrite `find_cardinal` (~`194-224`) to score a unified pool instead of splitting; keep `is_in_direction` + `score` as-is.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts` — mirror the same change in the JS shim so parity holds.
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/tests/parity.rs` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim-parity.test.ts` — add parity cases for the LeftNav→row-selector scenario and other cross-region moves so Rust and JS agree.
+
+### Out of scope
+
+- Changing the scoring formula itself (13:1 weighting). That's the Android constant; only touch it if the new unified ranking surfaces a clear case where it's wrong.
+- Introducing direction-dependent "major axis dominance" variants. Keep the algorithm a single formula.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] From a LeftNav view-switcher button, pressing Right lands on the nearest-visible scope in the main content area — specifically: in a grid view, the nearest row selector or leftmost body cell depending on geometry; in a board view, the leftmost column's first card or column header
+- [ ] From the first row selector of a grid, pressing Left lands on a LeftNav button (symmetry check)
+- [ ] From the perspective tab bar, pressing Down lands on the nearest content scope below (e.g. column header or first row selector) — currently `is_in_beam` for Down/Up requires horizontal overlap via the same formula; if it's broken the same way, fix too
+- [ ] In-beam candidates are still preferred when available — e.g. from a grid cell, Right still lands on the horizontally-adjacent cell in the same row, not on something out-of-beam
+- [ ] No regression in any existing spatial-nav test (Rust or JS)
+- [ ] Parity between Rust and JS shim maintained — every added parity case green on both sides
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Add Rust unit tests in `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` (or `tests/`) covering:
+  - `right_from_leftnav_reaches_grid_row_selector_when_no_in_beam_candidate_closer`
+  - `right_from_leftnav_prefers_in_beam_candidate_when_available` (in-beam preference regression)
+  - `left_from_grid_row_selector_reaches_leftnav`
+  - `down_from_perspective_bar_reaches_grid_header_when_no_horizontal_overlap`
+- [ ] Add the same cases to `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` — both Rust `tests/parity.rs` and JS `spatial-shim-parity.test.ts` must pass them identically
+- [ ] Add a vitest-browser test in `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-nav-leftnav.test.tsx` (or similar) that uses the real fixture shell: focus a LeftNav button, press Right, assert focus lands on a scope in the main content area (not on the LeftNav itself, not null)
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav` — all tests green
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all 1301+ tests + new tests green
+- [ ] Manual: open the app, click a LeftNav button, press Right → focus lands in the main view. Repeat from a grid row selector pressing Left → LeftNav button. Repeat from perspective tab pressing Down → content scope.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — start with the failing Rust unit tests at the algorithm level (fastest feedback), then extend to parity + vitest.
+- The algorithm lives in Rust; keep JS shim a faithful mirror via parity cases.
+- If the unified-ranking fix surfaces a regression in some direction (e.g. Up from a card lands somewhere unexpected), don't hot-patch — file a follow-up case in the parity JSON and adjust the scoring weights only if the test proves the current weighting is wrong.
+

changes.value:
## What

From the LeftNav (narrow left strip, near the top of the window), pressing Right doesn't reach the first visible grid row selector or board column. Same failure mode in other "cross-region" moves where the source rect doesn't vertically overlap any candidate-to-the-right that's "in the beam."

### Root cause

`swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs:118-128` — `is_in_beam`:

```rust
fn is_in_beam(source: &Rect, candidate: &Rect, direction: Direction) -> bool {
    match direction {
        Direction::Right | Direction::Left => {
            candidate.y < source.bottom() && candidate.bottom() > source.y
        }
        // ...
    }
}
```

For Left/Right, this requires **any** vertical overlap (≥1px) between source and candidate. If the LeftNav button is at `y:100-140` and the first grid row selector is at `y:200-236`, there's zero overlap — the row selector is **not** "in-beam."

`find_cardinal` (`spatial_nav.rs:194-224`) then pools candidates:

```rust
// simplified
let pool = if in_beam.is_empty() { &out_beam } else { &in_beam };
pool.iter().min_by_key(score)
```

The out-of-beam fallback **only fires when `in_beam.is_empty()`**. In practice, there's almost always at least one in-beam candidate to the right of the LeftNav (e.g. any toolbar button, header cell, or scope that happens to vertically overlap), so `in_beam` is non-empty → `out_beam` is silently discarded → the row selector (in `out_beam`) is never scored → Right from LeftNav lands on a random small target instead of the main content.

Same culling exists in the JS shim at `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts:136-148`, so the parity fence holds — both implementations are wrong together.

### Fix direction — score everything, weight in-beam

The pool-split is a hard cutoff that loses information. The scoring formula (`13 * major² + minor²` at `spatial_nav.rs:136-140`) already penalizes large minor-axis offsets heavily enough that an in-beam candidate with a small major distance will beat a vertically-distant out-of-beam candidate in most cases. The "in-beam wins even if absurdly far" behavior is the bug.

Replace the pool-split with a unified ranking:

1. All candidates that pass `is_in_direction` (strict: candidate's near edge >= source's far edge in the travel direction) are scored.
2. Scoring weights the minor axis: in-beam candidates score their beam-relative minor (center distance inside the overlap), out-of-beam candidates score the full center distance (unchanged — already the case at `spatial_nav.rs:140`).
3. Optionally, multiply out-of-beam scores by a penalty constant (e.g. 1.5×) to mildly prefer in-beam; but the minor-axis penalty usually dominates anyway.
4. Pick `min_by_key(score)` across the unified pool.

This preserves the preference for beam-aligned candidates (they score lower because minor is smaller) without starving cross-region moves when no in-beam candidate is close.

### Files to modify

- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` — rewrite `find_cardinal` (~`194-224`) to score a unified pool instead of splitting; keep `is_in_direction` + `score` as-is.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts` — mirror the same change in the JS shim so parity holds.
- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/tests/parity.rs` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim-parity.test.ts` — add parity cases for the LeftNav→row-selector scenario and other cross-region moves so Rust and JS agree.

### Out of scope

- Changing the scoring formula itself (13:1 weighting). That's the Android constant; only touch it if the new unified ranking surfaces a clear case where it's wrong.
- Introducing direction-dependent "major axis dominance" variants. Keep the algorithm a single formula.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] From a LeftNav view-switcher button, pressing Right lands on the nearest-visible scope in the main content area — specifically: in a grid view, the nearest row selector or leftmost body cell depending on geometry; in a board view, the leftmost column's first card or column header
- [ ] From the first row selector of a grid, pressing Left lands on a LeftNav button (symmetry check)
- [ ] From the perspective tab bar, pressing Down lands on the nearest content scope below (e.g. column header or first row selector) — currently `is_in_beam` for Down/Up requires horizontal overlap via the same formula; if it's broken the same way, fix too
- [ ] In-beam candidates are still preferred when available — e.g. from a grid cell, Right still lands on the horizontally-adjacent cell in the same row, not on something out-of-beam
- [ ] No regression in any existing spatial-nav test (Rust or JS)
- [ ] Parity between Rust and JS shim maintained — every added parity case green on both sides

## Tests

- [ ] Add Rust unit tests in `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` (or `tests/`) covering:
  - `right_from_leftnav_reaches_grid_row_selector_when_no_in_beam_candidate_closer`
  - `right_from_leftnav_prefers_in_beam_candidate_when_available` (in-beam preference regression)
  - `left_from_grid_row_selector_reaches_leftnav`
  - `down_from_perspective_bar_reaches_grid_header_when_no_horizontal_overlap`
- [ ] Add the same cases to `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` — both Rust `tests/parity.rs` and JS `spatial-shim-parity.test.ts` must pass them identically
- [ ] Add a vitest-browser test in `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-nav-leftnav.test.tsx` (or similar) that uses the real fixture shell: focus a LeftNav button, press Right, assert focus lands on a scope in the main content area (not on the LeftNav itself, not null)
- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav` — all tests green
- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — all 1301+ tests + new tests green
- [ ] Manual: open the app, click a LeftNav button, press Right → focus lands in the main view. Repeat from a grid row selector pressing Left → LeftNav button. Repeat from perspective tab pressing Down → content scope.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — start with the failing Rust unit tests at the algorithm level (fastest feedback), then extend to parity + vitest.
- The algorithm lives in Rust; keep JS shim a faithful mirror via parity cases.
- If the unified-ranking fix surfaces a regression in some direction (e.g. Up from a card lands somewhere unexpected), don't hot-patch — file a follow-up case in the parity JSON and adjust the scoring weights only if the test proves the current weighting is wrong.
Spatial nav: beam test starves cross-region moves — score all directional candidates, not just in-beam

-- [ ] From a LeftNav view-switcher button, pressing Right lands on the nearest-visible scope in the main content area — specifically: in a grid view, the nearest row selector or leftmost body cell depending on geometry; in a board view, the leftmost column's first card or column header
-- [ ] From the first row selector of a grid, pressing Left lands on a LeftNav button (symmetry check)
-- [ ] From the perspective tab bar, pressing Down lands on the nearest content scope below (e.g. column header or first row selector) — currently `is_in_beam` for Down/Up requires horizontal overlap via the same formula; if it's broken the same way, fix too
-- [ ] In-beam candidates are still preferred when available — e.g. from a grid cell, Right still lands on the horizontally-adjacent cell in the same row, not on something out-of-beam
-- [ ] No regression in any existing spatial-nav test (Rust or JS)
-- [ ] Parity between Rust and JS shim maintained — every added parity case green on both sides

 ## Workflow

 ```

-For Left/Right, this requires **any** vertical overlap (>=1px) between source and candidate. If the LeftNav button is at `y:100-140` and the first grid row selector is at `y:200-236`, there's zero overlap — the row selector is **not** "in-beam."
+For Left/Right, this requires **any** vertical overlap (≥1px) between source and candidate. If the LeftNav button is at `y:100-140` and the first grid row selector is at `y:200-236`, there's zero overlap — the row selector is **not** "in-beam."

 `find_cardinal` (`spatial_nav.rs:194-224`) then pools candidates:

@@ -35,53 +35,59 @@
 pool.iter().min_by_key(score)
 ```

-The out-of-beam fallback **only fires when `in_beam.is_empty()`**. In practice, there's almost always at least one in-beam candidate to the right of the LeftNav (e.g. any toolbar button, header cell, or scope that happens to vertically overlap), so `in_beam` is non-empty, `out_beam` is silently discarded, the row selector (in `out_beam`) is never scored, and Right from LeftNav lands on a random small target instead of the main content.
+The out-of-beam fallback **only fires when `in_beam.is_empty()`**. In practice, there's almost always at least one in-beam candidate to the right of the LeftNav (e.g. any toolbar button, header cell, or scope that happens to vertically overlap), so `in_beam` is non-empty → `out_beam` is silently discarded → the row selector (in `out_beam`) is never scored → Right from LeftNav lands on a random small target instead of the main content.

 Same culling exists in the JS shim at `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts:136-148`, so the parity fence holds — both implementations are wrong together.

-### Fix
+### Fix direction — score everything, weight in-beam

-Adopt the Android FocusFinder "beam beats" arbitration rule instead of a hard pool-split cutoff:
+The pool-split is a hard cutoff that loses information. The scoring formula (`13 * major² + minor²` at `spatial_nav.rs:136-140`) already penalizes large minor-axis offsets heavily enough that an in-beam candidate with a small major distance will beat a vertically-distant out-of-beam candidate in most cases. The "in-beam wins even if absurdly far" behavior is the bug.

-1. Score the best candidate within each pool (in-beam, out-of-beam).
-2. If both exist, the in-beam winner is preferred **unless** the out-of-beam winner is dramatically closer — specifically, unless its far edge is nearer than the in-beam winner's near edge along the travel direction.
+Replace the pool-split with a unified ranking:

-This preserves in-beam preference for normal same-row/same-column moves (grid cell right lands on the next cell), while unblocking cross-region moves when the nearest in-beam hit is absurdly far (LeftNav right lands on the first visible content scope instead of a far-right toolbar pill).
+1. All candidates that pass `is_in_direction` (strict: candidate's near edge >= source's far edge in the travel direction) are scored.
+2. Scoring weights the minor axis: in-beam candidates score their beam-relative minor (center distance inside the overlap), out-of-beam candidates score the full center distance (unchanged — already the case at `spatial_nav.rs:140`).
+3. Optionally, multiply out-of-beam scores by a penalty constant (e.g. 1.5×) to mildly prefer in-beam; but the minor-axis penalty usually dominates anyway.
+4. Pick `min_by_key(score)` across the unified pool.

-### Files modified
+This preserves the preference for beam-aligned candidates (they score lower because minor is smaller) without starving cross-region moves when no in-beam candidate is close.

-- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` — `find_cardinal` now scores both pools and arbitrates via `in_beam_dominates` (major-to-near vs major-to-far-edge comparison).
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts` — mirrored the same arbitration in the JS shim.
-- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` tests + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` — four new scenarios: `right_from_leftnav_reaches_grid_row_selector_when_no_in_beam_candidate_closer`, `right_from_leftnav_prefers_in_beam_candidate_when_available`, `left_from_grid_row_selector_reaches_leftnav`, `down_from_perspective_bar_reaches_grid_header_when_no_horizontal_overlap`.
+### Files to modify
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` — rewrite `find_cardinal` (~`194-224`) to score a unified pool instead of splitting; keep `is_in_direction` + `score` as-is.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts` — mirror the same change in the JS shim so parity holds.
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/tests/parity.rs` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim-parity.test.ts` — add parity cases for the LeftNav→row-selector scenario and other cross-region moves so Rust and JS agree.

 ### Out of scope

-- Changing the scoring formula itself (13:1 weighting). That's the Android constant; only touch it if the new arbitration surfaces a clear case where it's wrong.
+- Changing the scoring formula itself (13:1 weighting). That's the Android constant; only touch it if the new unified ranking surfaces a clear case where it's wrong.
 - Introducing direction-dependent "major axis dominance" variants. Keep the algorithm a single formula.

+This preserves the preference for beam-aligned candidates (they score lower because minor is smaller) without starving cross-region moves when no in-beam candidate is close.

-- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` — `find_cardinal` now scores both pools and arbitrates via `in_beam_dominates` (major-to-near vs major-to-far-edge comparison).
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts` — mirrored the same arbitration in the JS shim.
-- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` tests + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` — four new scenarios: `right_from_leftnav_reaches_grid_row_selector_when_no_in_beam_candidate_closer`, `right_from_leftnav_prefers_in_beam_candidate_when_available`, `left_from_grid_row_selector_reaches_leftnav`, `down_from_perspective_bar_reaches_grid_header_when_no_horizontal_overlap`.
+### Files to modify
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs` — rewrite `find_cardinal` (~`194-224`) to score a unified pool instead of splitting; keep `is_in_direction` + `score` as-is.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim.ts` — mirror the same change in the JS shim so parity holds.
+- `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/tests/parity.rs` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` + `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shim-parity.test.ts` — add parity cases for the LeftNav→row-selector scenario and other cross-region moves so Rust and JS agree.

 ### Out of scope

-- Changing the scoring formula itself (13:1 weighting). That's the Android constant; only touch it if the new arbitration surfaces a clear case where it's wrong.
+- Changing the scoring formula itself (13:1 weighting). That's the Android constant; only touch it if the new unified ranking surfaces a clear case where it's wrong.
 - Introducing direction-dependent "major axis dominance" variants. Keep the algorithm a single formula.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [x] From a LeftNav view-switcher button, pressing Right lands on the nearest-visible scope in the main content area — unit test + parity test + existing `spatial-nav-leftnav.test.tsx` `l` test all green
-- [x] From the first row selector of a grid, pressing Left lands on a LeftNav button (symmetry check) — unit test + parity test
-- [x] From the perspective tab bar, pressing Down lands on the nearest content scope below — unit test + parity test
-- [x] In-beam candidates are still preferred when available — `beam_candidate_preferred_over_closer_out_of_beam` + `right_from_leftnav_prefers_in_beam_candidate_when_available` green
-- [x] No regression in any existing spatial-nav test (Rust or JS) — all 66 Rust unit tests + 24 parity cases + 61 browser spatial-nav tests green
-- [x] Parity between Rust and JS shim maintained — 4 new parity cases green on both sides

+- [ ] From the first row selector of a grid, pressing Left lands on a LeftNav button (symmetry check)
+- [ ] From the perspective tab bar, pressing Down lands on the nearest content scope below (e.g. column header or first row selector) — currently `is_in_beam` for Down/Up requires horizontal overlap via the same formula; if it's broken the same way, fix too
+- [ ] In-beam candidates are still preferred when available — e.g. from a grid cell, Right still lands on the horizontally-adjacent cell in the same row, not on something out-of-beam
+- [ ] No regression in any existing spatial-nav test (Rust or JS)
+- [ ] Parity between Rust and JS shim maintained — every added parity case green on both sides

   - `down_from_perspective_bar_reaches_grid_header_when_no_horizontal_overlap`
-- [x] Added the same 4 cases to `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-parity-cases.json` — pass in both `tests/parity.rs` and `spatial-shim-parity.test.ts`
-- [x] `spatial-nav-leftnav.test.tsx` already covered the `l` (Right) from LeftNav to main body scope; that test passes against the new algorithm
-- [x] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav` — 66 unit tests + 1 parity test all green
-- [x] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — 1356 tests green (the pre-existing `nav-bar.test.tsx` module-resolution failure is unrelated; touched neither that file nor any file it imports)

+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav` — all tests green

 ## Workflow

-- Used TDD — wrote the 4 failing Rust unit tests first, watched 3 fail (4th was a regression guard), implemented the fix, then mirrored in the shim + parity JSON.
-- The algorithm lives in Rust; the JS shim is a faithful mirror verified by the shared parity fixture.
+- Use `/tdd` — start with the failing Rust unit tests at the algorithm level (fastest feedback), then extend to parity + vitest.
+- The algorithm lives in Rust; keep JS shim a faithful mirror via parity cases.
+- If the unified-ranking fix surfaces a regression in some direction (e.g. Up from a card lands somewhere unexpected), don't hot-patch — file a follow-up case in the parity JSON and adjust the scoring weights only if the test proves the current weighting is wrong.
